---
title: "NanoString Analysis March 2024"
author: "Rebecca Valls"
date: "2024-03-16"
output: html_document
---

#Download packages
```{r}

# Install the packages
install.packages("BiocManager")
BiocManager::install("limma")
BiocManager::install("lmTest")



install.packages("ggplot2")
install.packages("readr")
install.packages("lme4")
install.packages("sjPlot")
install.packages("dplyr")
install.packages("lmtest")
install.packages("tidyr")
install.packages("tidyverse")
install.packages("RColorBrewer")
install.packages("viridis")




library(ggplot2)
library(readr)
library(lme4)
library(sjPlot)
library(dplyr)
library(lmtest)
library(tidyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(limma)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(viridis)
library(ggrepel)








```
#Call in and format data

```{r}
NormalizedHostResponseCounts_failedsamplesrm <- read_csv("NormalizedHostResponseCounts_failedsamplesrm.csv")



#Format data

#make long format for easier stats and plotting
Nano_normalized_long_df <- pivot_longer(NormalizedHostResponseCounts_failedsamplesrm, 
                               cols = 8:806, 
                               names_to = "Gene", 
                               values_to = "NormalizedCount")


Nano_normalized_long_df$NormalizedCount <- as.numeric(as.character(Nano_normalized_long_df$NormalizedCount))

# Replace all non-alphanumeric characters with '_'
Nano_normalized_long_df$Gene <- gsub("[^a-zA-Z0-9]", "_", Nano_normalized_long_df$Gene)
Nano_normalized_long_df <- Nano_normalized_long_df[!grepl("NEG_|POS_", Nano_normalized_long_df$Gene), ]


#Subset the data to only have treatments Baseline and PBS
Nano_normalized_BasevsPBS <- subset(Nano_normalized_long_df, Treatment %in% c("Baseline", "PBS"))

#Subset the data to only have treatments PBS and Bacteroides
Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt <- subset(Nano_normalized_long_df, Treatment %in% c("PBS", "Bacteroides"))

#Subset the data to only have treatments Baseline and Bacteroides
Nano_normalized_BasevsPBS <- subset(Nano_normalized_long_df, Treatment %in% c("Baseline", "Bacteroides"))

#Factor Treatment
Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt$Treatment <- factor(Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt$Treatment, levels = c("PBS", "Bacteroides"))

#Subset the data to only have treatments PBS and Bacteroides for SI
Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_SI <- subset(Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt, Biogeography %in% c("SI"))

#Subset the data to only have treatments PBS and Bacteroides for Cec
Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_Cec <- subset(Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt, Biogeography %in% c("Cec"))

#Subset the data to only have treatments PBS and Bacteroides for Proximal Colon
Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_Pro <- subset(Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt, Biogeography %in% c("Pro"))

#Subset the data to only have treatments PBS and Bacteroides for Distal Colon
Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_Dis <- subset(Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt, Biogeography %in% c("Dis"))


#Subset the data to only have treatments PBS and Bacteroides for SI
Nano_normalized_BasevsPBS_SI <- subset(Nano_normalized_BasevsPBS, Biogeography %in% c("SI"))

#Subset the data to only have treatments PBS and Bacteroides for Cec
Nano_normalized_BasevsPBS_Cec <- subset(Nano_normalized_BasevsPBS, Biogeography %in% c("Cec"))

#Subset the data to only have treatments PBS and Bacteroides for Proximal Colon
Nano_normalized_BasevsPBS_Pro <- subset(Nano_normalized_BasevsPBS, Biogeography %in% c("Pro"))

#Subset the data to only have treatments PBS and Bacteroides for Distal Colon
Nano_normalized_BasevsPBS_Dis <- subset(Nano_normalized_BasevsPBS, Biogeography %in% c("Dis"))



```

#Call in NanoString Host Response Panel Data so that we can include more info on the probes and their pathways
```{r}

MouseHostResponsePanel_annotations <- read_csv("MouseHostResponsePanel_annotations.csv")

annotations_df <- MouseHostResponsePanel_annotations %>%
  gather(key = "Pathway", value = "Indicator", 4:60) %>%
  filter(Indicator == "+") %>%
  select(Gene, CellType = "Cell Type", Pathway) %>% # Change Cell_Type to your actual column name
  group_by(Gene, CellType) %>%
  summarise(Pathways = paste(Pathway, collapse = ", "), .groups = 'drop')



```

#Post GGplot of all counts (y axis) for each sample in the x axis
```{r}

# Sum the 'NormalizedCount' for each 'ShortLabel' and 'Treatment'.
Nano_normalized_sum_df <- Nano_normalized_long_df %>%
  group_by(ShortLabel, Treatment, Biogeography) %>%
  summarise(TotalNormalizedCount = sum(NormalizedCount)) %>%
  ungroup()

Nano_normalized_sum_df$Treatment <- factor(Nano_normalized_sum_df$Treatment, levels = c("Baseline", "PBS", "Bacteroides"))
Nano_normalized_sum_df$Biogeography <- factor(Nano_normalized_sum_df$Biogeography, levels = c("SI", "Cec", "Pro", "Dis"))


reads_across_samples_boxplot <- ggplot(Nano_normalized_sum_df, aes(x = Biogeography, y = TotalNormalizedCount, color = Treatment)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(y = "Total Reads", x = "Sample", color = "Treatment") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))  # Optionally rotate x-axis labels

reads_across_samples_boxplot

reads_across_samples_norm_boxplot <- ggplot(Nano_normalized_sum_df, aes(x = Treatment, y = TotalNormalizedCount, color = Treatment)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme_bw() +
  facet_wrap(~Biogeography, ncol=4)+
  labs(y = "Total Reads", x = "Sample", color = "Treatment") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange")) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))  # Optionally rotate x-axis labels

reads_across_samples_norm_boxplot

# Save the plot as a PDF
ggsave("reads_across_samples_norm_boxplot.pdf", plot = reads_across_samples_norm_boxplot, width = 8, height = 4, device = "pdf")



```



#Test linear model on one gene

```{r}
#Subset Nano_normalized_long_df to only have the KC gene (Cxcl1)

Nano_normalized_Cxcl1 <- subset(Nano_normalized_long_df, Gene == "Cxcl1")


# Fit the mixed linear model
Cxcl1_model <- lm(NormalizedCount ~ Treatment + Sex + Biogeography + (1|Mouse) , data = Nano_normalized_Cxcl1)

# Obtain the summary of the model
summary(Cxcl1_model)

#Subset just SI
Nano_normalized_Cxcl1_SI <- subset(Nano_normalized_Cxcl1, Biogeography == "SI")

# Fit the mixed linear model
Cxcl1_SI_model <- lm(NormalizedCount ~ Treatment + Sex + Treatment*Sex+(1|Mouse) , data = Nano_normalized_Cxcl1_SI)

# Obtain the summary of the model
summary(Cxcl1_SI_model)


```

#Make a boxplot for each gene, and print it onto a pdf page with Biogeography on the x axis, with each treatment colored, and each point a circle for sex female or triangle for sex male

```{r}
#Factor Biogeography
Nano_normalized_long_df$Biogeography <- factor(Nano_normalized_long_df$Biogeography, levels = c("SI", "Cec", "Pro", "Dis"))

#Factor Biogeography
Nano_normalized_long_df$Treatment <- factor(Nano_normalized_long_df$Treatment, levels = c("Baseline", "PBS", "Bacteroides"))

# Assuming Nano_normalized_long_df is already loaded in your R session

# Open a PDF device for plotting
pdf("Nano_normalized_genes_plots.pdf")

# Loop through each unique gene and create a boxplot
for(gene in unique(Nano_normalized_long_df$Gene)) {
  gene_data <- subset(Nano_normalized_long_df, Gene == gene)
  
  # Create the boxplot with ggplot
  p <- ggplot(gene_data, aes(x = Biogeography, y = NormalizedCount, color = Treatment)) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    geom_point(aes(shape = Sex), position=position_dodge(width=0.75)) +
    labs(title = gene) +
    scale_color_viridis_d() +
    scale_shape_manual(values = c("Female" = 16, "Male" = 17)) + # 16: circle, 17: triangle
    theme_bw()
  
  # Print the plot to the current page
  print(p)
}

# Close the PDF device
dev.off()

```



#Run a mixed effects linear model on each gene that determines what genes treatment has the largest effct on their count

#Just in the SI
#Just in the Cec
#Just in Pro
#Just in Dis
#Do this across all regions at once.
```{r}

# Calculate the number of unique values in each column
num_unique_values <- sapply(Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_SI, function(column) length(unique(column)))

# Print the number of unique values
print(num_unique_values)

# Ensure the 'linear_models_gene' directory exists
dir.create("linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI", showWarnings = FALSE)

# Initialize the summary dataframe
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_summary <- data.frame(
  Gene = character(),
  P_Value_Treatment = numeric(),
  P_Value_Sex = numeric(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Function to safely extract p-value
safe_extract_pvalue <- function(model_PBSvsBt_SI_summary, term) {
  coef_table <- as.data.frame(model_PBSvsBt_SI_summary$coefficients)
  if (term %in% rownames(coef_table)) {
    return(coef_table[term, "Pr(>|t|)"])
  }
  return(NA)
}

# Loop over each unique gene
for(gene in unique(Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_SI$Gene)) {
  # Subset data for the current gene
  subset_data <- subset(Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_SI, Gene == gene)

  # Fit the mixed linear model
  model <- lm(log2(NormalizedCount) ~ Treatment + Sex + Treatment*Sex, data = subset_data)

  # Obtain the summary of the model
  model_PBSvsBt_SI_summary <- summary(model)

  # Extract the p-values for Treatment and Sex
  p_treatment <- safe_extract_pvalue(model_PBSvsBt_SI_summary, "TreatmentBacteroides")
  p_sex <- safe_extract_pvalue(model_PBSvsBt_SI_summary, "SexMale")
  p_sex_treatment <- safe_extract_pvalue(model_BasevsPBS_SI_summary, "TreatmentBacteroides:SexMale")

  # Add to the summary dataframe
  linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_summary <- rbind(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_summary, data.frame(
    Gene = gene,
    P_Value_Treatment = p_treatment,
    P_Value_Sex = p_sex,
    P_Value_Sex_Treatment = p_sex_treatment
  ))

  # Save the summary of each model to a text file
  file_name <- paste0("linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI/model_", gene, ".txt")
  capture.output(model_PBSvsBt_SI_summary, file = file_name)
}

# View the summary dataframe
print(model_PBSvsBt_SI_summary)

# Function to categorize significance
categorize_significance <- function(p_value) {
  if(p_value < 0.001) {
    return("***")
  } else if(p_value < 0.01) {
    return("**")
  } else if(p_value < 0.05) {
    return("*")
  } else {
    return("NS")
  }
}

# Apply the function to the dataframe
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_summary$Significance_Treatment <- sapply(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_summary$P_Value_Treatment, categorize_significance)

# View the updated dataframe
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_summary

# Apply Bonferroni correction to the P_Value_Treatment column
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_summary$P_Value_Treatment_Bonferroni <- p.adjust(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_summary$P_Value_Treatment, method = "bonferroni")

linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_summary$Significance_Treatment_Bonferonni <- sapply(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_summary$P_Value_Treatment_Bonferroni, categorize_significance)

#Add annotations and cell type to p value summary
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_annotated_summary <- merge(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_summary, annotations_df, by = "Gene")

# Save the dataframe as a CSV file
write.csv(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_annotated_summary, "linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)

#FDR Correction
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_summary$FDRadjusted_p_value <- p.adjust(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_summary$P_Value_Treatment, method = "BH")



```


# Make a volcano plot that identifies genes significantly differentially expressed in Bt vs PBS treatment in the SI
https://biostatsquid.com/volcano-plots-r-tutorial/
```{r}
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_summary <- read_csv("linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_summary.csv")


# Filter data for nonCF and CF
SI_PBS_data <- Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_SI[Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_SI$Treatment == 'PBS', ]
SI_Bt_data <- Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_SI[Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_SI$Treatment == 'Bacteroides', ]

# Calculate median values for each cytokine in both PBS and Bt groups
median_PBS_SI <- aggregate(NormalizedCount ~ Gene, SI_PBS_data, median)
median_Bt_SI <- aggregate(NormalizedCount ~ Gene, SI_Bt_data, median)


# Merge the two dataframes on cytokine names
foldchange_SI_genes <- merge(median_PBS_SI, median_Bt_SI, by = "Gene")

# Calculate fold changes
foldchange_SI_genes$FoldChange <- foldchange_SI_genes$NormalizedCount.y / foldchange_SI_genes$NormalizedCount.x

# Rename columns for clarity
names(foldchange_SI_genes) <- c("Gene", "median_PBS", "median_Bt", "FoldChange")


# Merge the 'P_Value_Genotype' column from 'linear_models_serum_summary' into 'foldchange_serum'
foldchange_SI_genes <- merge(foldchange_SI_genes, linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_summary[, c("Gene", "P_Value_Treatment")], by = "Gene", all.x = TRUE)



```

#Draw a volcano plot on PBS vs Bt SI genes 
```{r}


# Transform the data
foldchange_SI_genes$log2FoldChange <- log2(foldchange_SI_genes$FoldChange)
foldchange_SI_genes$negLog10PValue <- -log10(foldchange_SI_genes$P_Value_Treatment)

# Add a new column "DiffExpression" to the foldchange_serum DataFrame
foldchange_SI_genes$DiffExpression <- ifelse(foldchange_SI_genes$P_Value_Treatment< 0.05, 
                                          ifelse(foldchange_SI_genes$FoldChange > 1, "Up in Bt", 
                                                 ifelse(foldchange_SI_genes$FoldChange < 1, "Down in Bt", "Not Significant")),
                                          "Not Significant")

# Biostatsquid theme
theme_set(theme_classic(base_size = 20) +
            theme(
              axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold", margin = margin(0,20,0,0), size = rel(0.5), color = 'black'),
              axis.title.x = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", margin = margin(20,0,0,0), size = rel(0.5), color = 'black'),
              plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
            ))

# Create a new data frame containing only the top 10 genes by negLog10PValue
top_10_genes_SI <- foldchange_SI_genes %>%
  slice_max(order_by = negLog10PValue, n = 10)

# You can print this to double-check
print(top_10_genes_SI)

# Create a basic volcano plot
Volcanoplot_PBSvsBt_SI <- ggplot(data = foldchange_SI_genes, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = negLog10PValue, color = DiffExpression)) +
  geom_point() +
  theme_bw() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-0.6, 0.6), col = "gray", linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), col = "gray", linetype = 'dashed') +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Up in Bt" = "#00AFBB", "Down in Bt" = "#bb0c00", "Not Significant" = "grey")) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 12)) +
  xlab(expression(log[2]("Fold Change"))) +  # x-axis label with subscript
  ylab(expression(-log[10]("P-Value"))) +

  # --- ADD THIS LAYER FOR LABELS ---
  geom_text_repel(
    data = top_10_genes_SI,         # Use the new data frame for labels
    aes(label = Gene),          # Tell ggplot that the 'Gene' column is the label
    size = 3.5,                 # Adjust font size
    box.padding = 1,          # Increase space between label and point
    max.overlaps = Inf,         # Ensure all labels are shown
    color = "black"             # Set a contrasting color for the labels
  )  

Volcanoplot_PBSvsBt_SI

# Save the plot as a PDF
ggsave("Volcanoplot_PBSvsBt_SI.pdf", plot = Volcanoplot_PBSvsBt_SI, width = 8, height = 4, device = "pdf")

# Count the number of genes in each category
diff_expression_counts <- table(foldchange_SI_genes$DiffExpression)

# Extract counts for "Up in Bt" and "Down in Bt"
up_in_bt_count <- diff_expression_counts["Up in Bt"]
down_in_bt_count <- diff_expression_counts["Down in Bt"]

# Print the counts
cat("Number of genes significantly upregulated in Bt: ", up_in_bt_count, "\n")
cat("Number of genes significantly downregulated in Bt: ", down_in_bt_count, "\n")



```


```{r}
# Ensure the 'linear_models_gene' directory exists
dir.create("linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec", showWarnings = FALSE)

# Initialize the summary dataframe
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary <- data.frame(
  Gene = character(),
  P_Value_Treatment = numeric(),
  P_Value_Sex = numeric(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Function to safely extract p-value
safe_extract_pvalue <- function(model_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary, term) {
  coef_table <- as.data.frame(model_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary$coefficients)
  if (term %in% rownames(coef_table)) {
    return(coef_table[term, "Pr(>|t|)"])
  }
  return(NA)
}

# Loop over each unique gene
for(gene in unique(Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_Cec$Gene)) {
  # Subset data for the current gene
  subset_data <- subset(Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_Cec, Gene == gene)

  # Fit the mixed linear model
  model <- lm(log2(NormalizedCount) ~ Treatment + Sex + Treatment*Sex, data = subset_data)

  # Obtain the summary of the model
  model_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary <- summary(model)

  # Extract the p-values for Treatment and Sex
  p_treatment <- safe_extract_pvalue(model_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary, "TreatmentBacteroides")
  p_sex <- safe_extract_pvalue(model_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary, "SexMale")
  p_treatment_sex <- safe_extract_pvalue(model_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary, "TreatmentBacteroides:SexMale")

  # Add to the summary dataframe
  linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary <- rbind(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary, data.frame(
    Gene = gene,
    P_Value_Treatment = p_treatment,
    P_Value_Sex = p_sex,
    P_Value_Treatment_Sex = p_treatment_sex
  ))

  # Save the summary of each model to a text file
  file_name <- paste0("linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec/model_", gene, ".txt")
  capture.output(model_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary, file = file_name)
}

# View the summary dataframe
print(model_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary)

# Function to categorize significance
categorize_significance <- function(p_value) {
  if(p_value < 0.001) {
    return("***")
  } else if(p_value < 0.01) {
    return("**")
  } else if(p_value < 0.05) {
    return("*")
  } else {
    return("NS")
  }
}

# Apply the function to the dataframe
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary$Significance_Treatment <- sapply(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary$P_Value_Treatment, categorize_significance)
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary$Significance_Sex <- sapply(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary$P_Value_Sex, categorize_significance)
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary$Significance_Treatment_Sex <- sapply(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary$P_Value_Treatment_Sex, categorize_significance)

# View the updated dataframe
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary

# Apply Bonferroni correction to the P_Value_Treatment column
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary$P_Value_Treatment_Bonferroni <- p.adjust(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary$P_Value_Treatment, method = "bonferroni")

linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary$P_Value_Sex_Bonferroni <- p.adjust(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary$P_Value_Sex, method = "bonferroni")

linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary$Significance_Treatment_Bonferonni <- sapply(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary$P_Value_Treatment_Bonferroni, categorize_significance)

linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary$Significance_Sex_Bonferonni <- sapply(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary$P_Value_Sex_Bonferroni, categorize_significance)



#Add annotations and cell type to p value summary
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_annotated_summary <- merge(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary, annotations_df, by = "Gene")

# Save the dataframe as a CSV file
write.csv(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_annotated_summary, "linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)


#FDR Correction
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary$FDRadjusted_p_value <- p.adjust(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary$P_Value_Treatment, method = "BH")


```

# Make a volcano plot that identifies genes significantly differentially expressed in Bt vs PBS treatment in the Cec
https://biostatsquid.com/volcano-plots-r-tutorial/
```{r}

linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary <- read_csv("linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary.csv")


# Filter data for PBS and Bt
Cec_PBS_data <- Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_Cec[Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_Cec$Treatment == 'PBS', ]
Cec_Bt_data <- Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_Cec[Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_Cec$Treatment == 'Bacteroides', ]

# Calculate median values for each cytokine in both PBS and Bt groups
median_PBS_Cec <- aggregate(NormalizedCount ~ Gene, Cec_PBS_data, median)
median_Bt_Cec <- aggregate(NormalizedCount ~ Gene, Cec_Bt_data, median)


# Merge the two dataframes on cytokine names
foldchange_Cec_genes <- merge(median_PBS_Cec, median_Bt_Cec, by = "Gene")

# Calculate fold changes
foldchange_Cec_genes$FoldChange <- foldchange_Cec_genes$NormalizedCount.y / foldchange_Cec_genes$NormalizedCount.x

# Rename columns for clarity
names(foldchange_Cec_genes) <- c("Gene", "median_PBS", "median_Bt", "FoldChange")


# Merge the 'P_Value_Genotype' column from 'linear_models_serum_summary' into 'foldchange_serum'
foldchange_Cec_genes <- merge(foldchange_Cec_genes, linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary[, c("Gene", "P_Value_Treatment")], by = "Gene", all.x = TRUE)



```

#Draw a volcano plot on PBS vs Bt Cec genes 
```{r}
# Transform the data
foldchange_Cec_genes$log2FoldChange <- log2(foldchange_Cec_genes$FoldChange)
foldchange_Cec_genes$negLog10PValue <- -log10(foldchange_Cec_genes$P_Value_Treatment)

# Add a new column "DiffExpressicon" to the foldchange_serum DataFrame
foldchange_Cec_genes$DiffExpression <- ifelse(foldchange_Cec_genes$P_Value_Treatment< 0.05, 
                                          ifelse(foldchange_Cec_genes$FoldChange > 1, "Up in Bt", 
                                                 ifelse(foldchange_Cec_genes$FoldChange < 1, "Down in Bt", "Not Significant")),
                                          "Not Significant")

# Biostatsquid theme
theme_set(theme_classic(base_size = 20) +
            theme(
              axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold", margin = margin(0,20,0,0), size = rel(0.5), color = 'black'),
              axis.title.x = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", margin = margin(20,0,0,0), size = rel(0.5), color = 'black'),
              plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
            ))

# Create a new data frame containing only the top 10 genes by negLog10PValue
top_10_genes_Cec <- foldchange_Cec_genes %>%
  slice_max(order_by = negLog10PValue, n = 10)

# You can print this to double-check
print(top_10_genes_Cec)

# Create a basic volcano plot
Volcanoplot_PBSvsBt_Cec<- ggplot(data = foldchange_Cec_genes, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = negLog10PValue, color = DiffExpression)) +
         geom_point()+
  theme_bw()+
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-0.6, 0.6), col = "gray", linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), col = "gray", linetype = 'dashed') +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Up in Bt" = "#00AFBB", "Down in Bt" = "#bb0c00", "Not Significant" = "grey"))+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 12)) + # Reduced font size for title
  xlab(expression(log[2]("Fold Change"))) +  # x-axis label with subscript
  ylab(expression(-log[10]("P-Value"))) +
  
  # --- ADD THIS LAYER FOR LABELS ---
  geom_text_repel(
    data = top_10_genes_Cec,         # Use the new data frame for labels
    aes(label = Gene),          # Tell ggplot that the 'Gene' column is the label
    size = 3.5,                 # Adjust font size
    box.padding = 1,          # Increase space between label and point
    max.overlaps = Inf,         # Ensure all labels are shown
    color = "black"             # Set a contrasting color for the labels
  )  
  
  
Volcanoplot_PBSvsBt_Cec

# Save the plot as a PDF
ggsave("Volcanoplot_PBSvsBt_Cec.pdf", plot = Volcanoplot_PBSvsBt_Cec, width = 8, height = 4, device = "pdf")


# Count the number of genes in each category
diff_expression_counts_Cec <- table(foldchange_Cec_genes$DiffExpression)

# Extract counts for "Up in Bt" and "Down in Bt"
up_in_bt_count_Cec <- diff_expression_counts_Cec["Up in Bt"]
down_in_bt_count_Cec <- diff_expression_counts_Cec["Down in Bt"]

# Print the counts
cat("Number of genes significantly upregulated in Bt: ", up_in_bt_count_Cec, "\n")
cat("Number of genes significantly downregulated in Bt: ", down_in_bt_count_Cec, "\n")





```

```{r}
# Ensure the 'linear_models_gene' directory exists
dir.create("linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro", showWarnings = FALSE)

# Initialize the summary dataframe
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary <- data.frame(
  Gene = character(),
  P_Value_Treatment = numeric(),
  P_Value_Sex = numeric(),
  P_Value_TreatmentSex = numeric(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Function to safely extract p-value
safe_extract_pvalue <- function(model_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary, term) {
  coef_table <- as.data.frame(model_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary$coefficients)
  if (term %in% rownames(coef_table)) {
    return(coef_table[term, "Pr(>|t|)"])
  }
  return(NA)
}

# Loop over each unique gene
for(gene in unique(Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_Pro$Gene)) {
  # Subset data for the current gene
  subset_data <- subset(Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_Pro, Gene == gene)

  # Fit the mixed linear model
  model <- lm(log2(NormalizedCount) ~ Treatment + Sex + Sex*Treatment, data = subset_data)

  # Obtain the summary of the model
  model_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary <- summary(model)

  # Extract the p-values for Treatment and Sex
  p_treatment <- safe_extract_pvalue(model_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary, "TreatmentBacteroides")
  p_sex <- safe_extract_pvalue(model_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary, "SexMale")
  p_treatment_sex <- safe_extract_pvalue(model_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary, "TreatmentBacteroides:SexMale")

  # Add to the summary dataframe
  linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary <- rbind(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary, data.frame(
    Gene = gene,
    P_Value_Treatment = p_treatment,
    P_Value_Sex = p_sex,
    P_Value_Treatment_Sex = p_treatment_sex
  ))

  # Save the summary of each model to a text file
  file_name <- paste0("linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro/model_", gene, ".txt")
  capture.output(model_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary, file = file_name)
}

# View the summary dataframe
print(model_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary)

# Function to categorize significance
categorize_significance <- function(p_value) {
  # Check if p_value is NA
  if (is.na(p_value)) {
    return("NA")
  }

  if (p_value < 0.001) {
    return("***")
  } else if (p_value < 0.01) {
    return("**")
  } else if (p_value < 0.05) {
    return("*")
  } else {
    return("NS")
  }
}

# Apply the function to the dataframe
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary$Significance_Treatment <- sapply(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary$P_Value_Treatment, categorize_significance)

linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary$Significance_Sex <- sapply(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary$P_Value_Sex, categorize_significance)

linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary$Significance_Treatment_Sex <- sapply(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary$P_Value_Treatment_Sex, categorize_significance)


# View the updated dataframe
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary

# Apply Bonferroni correction to the P_Value_Treatment column
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary$P_Value_Treatment_Bonferroni <- p.adjust(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary$P_Value_Treatment, method = "bonferroni")


linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary$P_Value_Sex_Bonferroni <- p.adjust(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary$P_Value_Sex, method = "bonferroni")


linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary$P_Value_Treatmen_Sex_Bonferroni <- p.adjust(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary$P_Value_Treatment_Sex, method = "bonferroni")


#Add annotations and cell type to p value summary
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_annotated_summary <- merge(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary, annotations_df, by = "Gene")


# Save the dataframe as a CSV file
write.csv(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_annotated_summary, "linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)

#FDR Correction
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary$FDRadjusted_p_value <- p.adjust(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary$P_Value_Treatment, method = "BH")



```

# Make a volcano plot that identifies genes significantly differentially expressed in Bt vs PBS treatment in the Pro
https://biostatsquid.com/volcano-plots-r-tutorial/
```{r}

linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary <- read_csv("linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary.csv")

# Filter data for PBS and Bt
Pro_PBS_data <- Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_Pro[Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_Pro$Treatment == 'PBS', ]
Pro_Bt_data <- Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_Pro[Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_Pro$Treatment == 'Bacteroides', ]

# Calculate median values for each cytokine in both PBS and Bt groups
median_PBS_Pro <- aggregate(NormalizedCount ~ Gene, Pro_PBS_data, median)
median_Bt_Pro <- aggregate(NormalizedCount ~ Gene, Pro_Bt_data, median)


# Merge the two dataframes on cytokine names
foldchange_Pro_genes <- merge(median_PBS_Pro, median_Bt_Pro, by = "Gene")

# Calculate fold changes
foldchange_Pro_genes$FoldChange <- foldchange_Pro_genes$NormalizedCount.y / foldchange_Pro_genes$NormalizedCount.x

# Rename columns for clarity
names(foldchange_Pro_genes) <- c("Gene", "median_PBS", "median_Bt", "FoldChange")


# Merge the 'P_Value_Genotype' column from 'linear_models_serum_summary' into 'foldchange_serum'
foldchange_Pro_genes <- merge(foldchange_Pro_genes, linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary[, c("Gene", "P_Value_Treatment")], by = "Gene", all.x = TRUE)



```

#Draw a volcano plot on PBS vs Bt Pro genes 
```{r}
# Transform the data
foldchange_Pro_genes$log2FoldChange <- log2(foldchange_Pro_genes$FoldChange)
foldchange_Pro_genes$negLog10PValue <- -log10(foldchange_Pro_genes$P_Value_Treatment)

# Add a new column "DiffExpressicon" to the foldchange_serum DataFrame
foldchange_Pro_genes$DiffExpression <- ifelse(foldchange_Pro_genes$P_Value_Treatment< 0.05, 
                                          ifelse(foldchange_Pro_genes$FoldChange > 1, "Up in Bt", 
                                                 ifelse(foldchange_Pro_genes$FoldChange < 1, "Down in Bt", "Not Significant")),
                                          "Not Significant")

# Biostatsquid theme
theme_set(theme_classic(base_size = 20) +
            theme(
              axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold", margin = margin(0,20,0,0), size = rel(0.5), color = 'black'),
              axis.title.x = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", margin = margin(20,0,0,0), size = rel(0.5), color = 'black'),
              plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
            ))

# Create a new data frame containing only the top 10 genes by negLog10PValue
top_10_genes_Pro <- foldchange_Pro_genes %>%
  slice_max(order_by = negLog10PValue, n = 10)

# You can print this to double-check
print(top_10_genes_Pro)

# Create a basic volcano plot
Volcanoplot_PBSvsBt_Pro<- ggplot(data = foldchange_Pro_genes, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = negLog10PValue, color = DiffExpression)) +
         geom_point()+
  theme_bw()+
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-0.6, 0.6), col = "gray", linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), col = "gray", linetype = 'dashed') +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Up in Bt" = "#00AFBB", "Down in Bt" = "#bb0c00", "Not Significant" = "grey"))+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 12)) + # Reduced font size for title
  xlab(expression(log[2]("Fold Change"))) +  # x-axis label with subscript
  ylab(expression(-log[10]("P-Value"))) +
  
  # --- ADD THIS LAYER FOR LABELS ---
  geom_text_repel(
    data = top_10_genes_Pro,         # Use the new data frame for labels
    aes(label = Gene),          # Tell ggplot that the 'Gene' column is the label
    size = 3.5,                 # Adjust font size
    box.padding = 1,          # Increase space between label and point
    max.overlaps = Inf,         # Ensure all labels are shown
    color = "black"             # Set a contrasting color for the labels
  )  
Volcanoplot_PBSvsBt_Pro

# Save the plot as a PDF
ggsave("Volcanoplot_PBSvsBt_Pro.pdf", plot = Volcanoplot_PBSvsBt_Pro, width = 8, height = 4, device = "pdf")


# Count the number of genes in each category
diff_expression_counts_Pro <- table(foldchange_Pro_genes$DiffExpression)

# Extract counts for "Up in Bt" and "Down in Bt"
up_in_bt_count_Pro <- diff_expression_counts_Pro["Up in Bt"]
down_in_bt_count_Pro <- diff_expression_counts_Pro["Down in Bt"]

# Print the counts
cat("Number of genes significantly upregulated in Bt: ", up_in_bt_count_Pro, "\n")
cat("Number of genes significantly downregulated in Bt: ", down_in_bt_count_Pro, "\n")


```


```{r}
# Ensure the 'linear_models_gene' directory exists
dir.create("linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis", showWarnings = FALSE)

# Initialize the summary dataframe
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary <- data.frame(
  Gene = character(),
  P_Value_Treatment = numeric(),
  P_Value_Sex = numeric(),
  P_Value_Treatment_Sex = numeric(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Function to safely extract p-value
safe_extract_pvalue <- function(model_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary, term) {
  coef_table <- as.data.frame(model_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary$coefficients)
  if (term %in% rownames(coef_table)) {
    return(coef_table[term, "Pr(>|t|)"])
  }
  return(NA)
}

# Loop over each unique gene
for(gene in unique(Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_Dis$Gene)) {
  # Subset data for the current gene
  subset_data <- subset(Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_Dis, Gene == gene)

  # Fit the mixed linear model
  model <- lm(log2(NormalizedCount) ~ Treatment + Sex + Treatment*Sex, data = subset_data)

  # Obtain the summary of the model
  model_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary <- summary(model)

  # Extract the p-values for Treatment and Sex
  p_treatment <- safe_extract_pvalue(model_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary, "TreatmentBacteroides")
  p_sex <- safe_extract_pvalue(model_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary, "SexMale")
  p_treatment_sex <- safe_extract_pvalue(model_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary, "TreatmentBacteroides:SexMale")

  # Add to the summary dataframe
  linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary <- rbind(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary, data.frame(
    Gene = gene,
    P_Value_Treatment = p_treatment,
    P_Value_Sex = p_sex,
    P_Value_Treatment_Sex = p_treatment_sex
  ))

  # Save the summary of each model to a text file
  file_name <- paste0("linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis/model_", gene, ".txt")
  capture.output(model_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary, file = file_name)
}

# View the summary dataframe
print(model_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary)

# Function to categorize significance
categorize_significance <- function(p_value) {
  # Check if p_value is NA
  if (is.na(p_value)) {
    return("NA")
  }

  if (p_value < 0.001) {
    return("***")
  } else if (p_value < 0.01) {
    return("**")
  } else if (p_value < 0.05) {
    return("*")
  } else {
    return("NS")
  }
}

# Apply the function to the dataframe
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary$Significance_Treatment <- sapply(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary$P_Value_Treatment, categorize_significance)

linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary$Significance_Sex <- sapply(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary$P_Value_Sex, categorize_significance)

linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary$Significance_Treatment_Sex <- sapply(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary$P_Value_Treatment_Sex, categorize_significance)


# View the updated dataframe
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary

# Apply Bonferroni correction to the P_Value_Treatment column
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary$P_Value_Treatment_Bonferroni <- p.adjust(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary$P_Value_Treatment, method = "bonferroni")

linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary$Significance_Sex_Bonferonni <- p.adjust(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary$P_Value_Sex, method = "bonferroni")

linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary$Significance_Treatment_Sex_Bonferonni <- p.adjust(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary$P_Value_Treatment_Sex, method = "bonferroni")


#Add annotations and cell type to p value summary
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_annotated_summary <- merge(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary, annotations_df, by = "Gene")

# Save the dataframe as a CSV file
write.csv(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_annotated_summary, "linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)


#FDR Correction
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary$FDRadjusted_p_value <- p.adjust(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary$P_Value_Treatment, method = "BH")


```

# Make a volcano plot that identifies genes significantly differentially expressed in Bt vs PBS treatment in the Dis
https://biostatsquid.com/volcano-plots-r-tutorial/
```{r}

# Filter data for PBS and Bt
Dis_PBS_data <- Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_Dis[Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_Dis$Treatment == 'PBS', ]
Dis_Bt_data <- Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_Dis[Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt_Dis$Treatment == 'Bacteroides', ]

# Calculate median values for each cytokine in both PBS and Bt groups
median_PBS_Dis <- aggregate(NormalizedCount ~ Gene, Dis_PBS_data, median)
median_Bt_Dis <- aggregate(NormalizedCount ~ Gene, Dis_Bt_data, median)


# Merge the two dataframes on cytokine names
foldchange_Dis_genes <- merge(median_PBS_Dis, median_Bt_Dis, by = "Gene")

# Calculate fold changes
foldchange_Dis_genes$FoldChange <- foldchange_Dis_genes$NormalizedCount.y / foldchange_Dis_genes$NormalizedCount.x

# Rename columns for clarity
names(foldchange_Dis_genes) <- c("Gene", "median_PBS", "median_Bt", "FoldChange")


# Merge the 'P_Value_Genotype' column from 'linear_models_serum_summary' into 'foldchange_serum'
foldchange_Dis_genes <- merge(foldchange_Dis_genes, linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary[, c("Gene", "P_Value_Treatment")], by = "Gene", all.x = TRUE)



```

#Draw a volcano plot on PBS vs Bt Dis genes 
```{r}
# Transform the data
foldchange_Dis_genes$log2FoldChange <- log2(foldchange_Dis_genes$FoldChange)
foldchange_Dis_genes$negLog10PValue <- -log10(foldchange_Dis_genes$P_Value_Treatment)

# Add a new column "DiffExpressicon" to the foldchange_serum DataFrame
foldchange_Dis_genes$DiffExpression <- ifelse(foldchange_Dis_genes$P_Value_Treatment< 0.05, 
                                          ifelse(foldchange_Dis_genes$FoldChange > 1, "Up in Bt", 
                                                 ifelse(foldchange_Dis_genes$FoldChange < 1, "Down in Bt", "Not Significant")),
                                          "Not Significant")

# Biostatsquid theme
theme_set(theme_classic(base_size = 20) +
            theme(
              axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold", margin = margin(0,20,0,0), size = rel(0.5), color = 'black'),
              axis.title.x = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", margin = margin(20,0,0,0), size = rel(0.5), color = 'black'),
              plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
            ))

# Create a new data frame containing only the top 10 genes by negLog10PValue
top_10_genes_Dis <- foldchange_Dis_genes %>%
  slice_max(order_by = negLog10PValue, n = 10)

txk_only_df <- foldchange_Dis_genes %>% filter(Gene == "Txk")

top_10_genes_Dis <- top_10_genes_Dis %>% bind_rows(txk_only_df)

# You can print this to double-check
print(top_10_genes_Dis)

# Create a basic volcano plot
Volcanoplot_PBSvsBt_Dis<- ggplot(data = foldchange_Dis_genes, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = negLog10PValue, color = DiffExpression)) +
         geom_point()+
  theme_bw()+
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-0.6, 0.6), col = "gray", linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), col = "gray", linetype = 'dashed') +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Up in Bt" = "#00AFBB", "Down in Bt" = "#bb0c00", "Not Significant" = "grey"))+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 12)) + # Reduced font size for title
  
  # --- ADD THIS LAYER FOR LABELS ---
  geom_text_repel(
    data = top_10_genes_Dis,         # Use the new data frame for labels
    aes(label = Gene),          # Tell ggplot that the 'Gene' column is the label
    size = 3.5,                 # Adjust font size
    box.padding = 0.8,          # Increase space between label and point
    max.overlaps = Inf,         # Ensure all labels are shown
    color = "black"             # Set a contrasting color for the labels
  )  

Volcanoplot_PBSvsBt_Dis

# Save the plot as a PDF
ggsave("Volcanoplot_PBSvsBt_Dis.pdf", plot = Volcanoplot_PBSvsBt_Dis, width = 8, height = 4, device = "pdf")


# Count the number of genes in each category
diff_expression_counts_Dis <- table(foldchange_Dis_genes$DiffExpression)

# Extract counts for "Up in Bt" and "Down in Bt"
up_in_bt_count_Dis <- diff_expression_counts_Dis["Up in Bt"]
down_in_bt_count_Dis <- diff_expression_counts_Dis["Down in Bt"]

# Print the counts
cat("Number of genes significantly upregulated in Bt: ", up_in_bt_count_Dis, "\n")
cat("Number of genes significantly downregulated in Bt: ", down_in_bt_count_Dis, "\n")

```

#Make a table that outlines the Pathways that are regulated by Bacteroides in the SI
```{r}
# Assuming df is your dataframe
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_annotated_summary_filtered_df <- linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_annotated_summary[linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_annotated_summary$P_Value_Treatment <= 0.05, ]

# Splitting the Pathways column and unlisting it
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_annotated_summary_filtered_df_pathways_list <- unlist(strsplit(as.character(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_annotated_summary_filtered_df$Pathways), ", "))

# Counting occurrences of each pathway
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_annotated_summary_pathway_counts <- table(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_annotated_summary_filtered_df_pathways_list)

# Creating a new dataframe
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_annotated_summary_new_df <- data.frame(Pathway = names(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_annotated_summary_pathway_counts), Counts = as.integer(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_annotated_summary_pathway_counts))

```

#Make a table that outlines the Pathways that are regulated by Bacteroides in the Cecum
```{r}
# Assuming df is your dataframe
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_annotated_summary_filtered_df <- linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_annotated_summary[linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_annotated_summary$P_Value_Treatment <= 0.05, ]

# Splitting the Pathways column and unlisting it
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_annotated_summary_filtered_df_pathways_list <- unlist(strsplit(as.character(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_annotated_summary_filtered_df$Pathways), ", "))

# Counting occurrences of each pathway
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_annotated_summary_pathway_counts <- table(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_annotated_summary_filtered_df_pathways_list)

# Creating a new dataframe
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_annotated_summary_new_df <- data.frame(Pathway = names(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_annotated_summary_pathway_counts), Counts = as.integer(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_annotated_summary_pathway_counts))

```

#Make a table that outlines the Pathways that are regulated by Bacteroides in the Proximal Colon
```{r}
# Assuming df is your dataframe
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_annotated_summary_filtered_df <- linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_annotated_summary[linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_annotated_summary$P_Value_Treatment <= 0.05, ]

# Splitting the Pathways column and unlisting it
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_annotated_summary_filtered_df_pathways_list <- unlist(strsplit(as.character(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_annotated_summary_filtered_df$Pathways), ", "))

# Counting occurrences of each pathway
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_annotated_summary_pathway_counts <- table(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_annotated_summary_filtered_df_pathways_list)

# Creating a new dataframe
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_annotated_summary_new_df <- data.frame(Pathway = names(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_annotated_summary_pathway_counts), Counts = as.integer(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_annotated_summary_pathway_counts))

```

#Make a table that outlines the Pathways that are regulated by Bacteroides in the Dis Colon
```{r}
# Assuming df is your dataframe
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_annotated_summary_filtered_df <- linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_annotated_summary[linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_annotated_summary$P_Value_Treatment <= 0.05, ]

# Splitting the Pathways column and unlisting it
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_annotated_summary_filtered_df_pathways_list <- unlist(strsplit(as.character(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_annotated_summary_filtered_df$Pathways), ", "))

# Counting occurrences of each pathway
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_annotated_summary_pathway_counts <- table(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_annotated_summary_filtered_df_pathways_list)

# Creating a new dataframe
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_annotated_summary_new_df <- data.frame(Pathway = names(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_annotated_summary_pathway_counts), Counts = as.integer(linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_annotated_summary_pathway_counts))

```


#Generate a heatmap of region on the x axis and treatment groups clustered on y axis
```{r}
Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt

#Clustrer Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt on y axis based on what pathways they belong to
# Merge the data frames based on the 'Gene' column
merged_Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt <- Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt %>% 
  left_join(annotations_df, by = "Gene")



unique_values <- unique(Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt$ShortLabel)

#Factor Treatment
Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt$ShortLabel <- factor(Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt$ShortLabel, levels = c("PBS", "Bacteroides"))


#Make a heatmap of the SI
#Creating the heatmap
ggplot(Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt, aes(x = ShortLabel, y = Gene, fill = log2(NormalizedCount))) +
  geom_tile() +  # Use geom_tile for heatmap
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red") +  # Adjust color gradient
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(fill = "Normalized Count") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 5)) # Rotate x-axis labels if needed



pheatmap(Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt, 
         annotation_row=response,
         scale = 'row', 
         cluster_cols = FALSE, 
         cluster_rows = TRUE)


```

#Print a plot of Normalized Counts on y axis and the treatment groups on x axis, faceted by region

```{r}
#Merge the dataframe with annotationos
Nano_normalized_long_df_annotated <- merge(Nano_normalized_long_df, annotations_df, by = "Gene")


#subset the Nano_normalized_long_df dataframe to only include G couupled receptors, and toll receptors
# List of specific genes
genes_of_interest <- c("Adgre5", "Adgrg3", "Ticam1", "Tirap", "Tlr1", "Tlr2", "Tlr3", "Tlr4", "Tlr5", "Tlr6", "Tlr7", "Tlr8", "Tlr9")

# Subset the dataframe
Nano_normalized_long_df_annotated_receptors <- Nano_normalized_long_df_annotated[Nano_normalized_long_df_annotated$Gene %in% genes_of_interest, ]



#Factor Treatment and Biogeography
Nano_normalized_long_df_annotated_receptors$Treatment <- factor(Nano_normalized_long_df_annotated_receptors$Treatment, levels = c("PBS", "Bacteroides", "Baseline"))

Nano_normalized_long_df_annotated_receptors$Biogeography <- factor(Nano_normalized_long_df_annotated_receptors$Biogeography, levels = c("SI", "Cec", "Pro", "Dis"))


# Assuming your dataframe is named df
toll_like_receptors_acrossthegut <- ggplot(Nano_normalized_long_df_annotated_receptors, aes(x = Biogeography, y = NormalizedCount, color = Treatment)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~ Gene, scales = "free_y") +  # Free y-axis scale for each facet
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange")) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(y = "Normalized Count", x = "Biogeography", color = "Treatment") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))  # Optionally rotate x-axis labels


# Save the plot as a PDF
ggsave("toll_like_receptors_acrossthegut.pdf", plot = toll_like_receptors_acrossthegut, width = 8, height = 4, device = "pdf")



```


#Compare baseline to PBS
```{r}

Nano_normalized_BasevsPBS_SI$Treatment <- factor(Nano_normalized_BasevsPBS_SI$Treatment, levels = c("Baseline", "PBS"))
Nano_normalized_BasevsPBS_SI <- subset(Nano_normalized_BasevsPBS_SI, Treatment %in% c("Baseline", "PBS"))

# Print the unique values in the Treatment column
unique_values <- unique(Nano_normalized_BasevsPBS_SI$Treatment)
print(unique_values)

# Ensure the 'linear_models_gene' directory exists
dir.create("linear_models_genes_BasevsPBS_SI", showWarnings = FALSE)

# Initialize the summary dataframe
linear_models_genes_BasevsPBS_SI_summary <- data.frame(
  Gene = character(),
  P_Value_Treatment = numeric(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Function to safely extract p-value
safe_extract_pvalue <- function(model_BasevsPBS_SI_summary, term) {
  coef_table <- as.data.frame(model_BasevsPBS_SI_summary$coefficients)
  if (term %in% rownames(coef_table)) {
    return(coef_table[term, "Pr(>|t|)"])
  }
  return(NA)
}

# Loop over each unique gene
for(gene in unique(Nano_normalized_BasevsPBS_SI$Gene)) {
  # Subset data for the current gene
  subset_data <- subset(Nano_normalized_BasevsPBS_SI, Gene == gene)

  # Fit the mixed linear model
  model <- lm(log2(NormalizedCount) ~ Treatment, data = subset_data)

  # Obtain the summary of the model
  model_BasevsPBS_SI_summary <- summary(model)

  # Extract the p-values for Treatment and Sex
  p_treatment <- safe_extract_pvalue(model_BasevsPBS_SI_summary, "TreatmentPBS")

  # Add to the summary dataframe
  linear_models_genes_BasevsPBS_SI_summary <- rbind(linear_models_genes_BasevsPBS_SI_summary, data.frame(
    Gene = gene,
    P_Value_Treatment = p_treatment
  ))

  # Save the summary of each model to a text file
  file_name <- paste0("linear_models_genes_BasevsPBS_SI/model_", gene, ".txt")
  capture.output(model_BasevsPBS_SI_summary, file = file_name)
}

# View the summary dataframe
print(model_BasevsPBS_SI_summary)

# Function to categorize significance
categorize_significance <- function(p_value) {
  if(p_value < 0.001) {
    return("***")
  } else if(p_value < 0.01) {
    return("**")
  } else if(p_value < 0.05) {
    return("*")
  } else {
    return("NS")
  }
}

# Apply the function to the dataframe
linear_models_genes_BasevsPBS_SI_summary$Significance_Treatment <- sapply(linear_models_genes_BasevsPBS_SI_summary$P_Value_Treatment, categorize_significance)

# View the updated dataframe
linear_models_genes_BasevsPBS_SI_summary

# Apply Bonferroni correction to the P_Value_Treatment column
linear_models_genes_BasevsPBS_SI_summary$P_Value_Treatment_Bonferroni <- p.adjust(linear_models_genes_BasevsPBS_SI_summary$P_Value_Treatment, method = "bonferroni")

linear_models_genes_BasevsPBS_SI_summary$Significance_Treatment_Bonferonni <- sapply(linear_models_genes_BasevsPBS_SI_summary$P_Value_Treatment_Bonferroni, categorize_significance)

#Add annotations and cell type to p value summary
linear_models_genes_BasevsPBS_SI_annotated_summary <- merge(linear_models_genes_BasevsPBS_SI_summary, annotations_df, by = "Gene")

# Save the dataframe as a CSV file
write.csv(linear_models_genes_BasevsPBS_SI_annotated_summary, "linear_models_genes_BasevsPBS_SI_summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)





```


# Make a volcano plot that identifies genes significantly differentially expressed in Base vs PBS treatment in the SI
https://biostatsquid.com/volcano-plots-r-tutorial/
```{r}
linear_models_genes_BasevsPBS_SI_summary <- read_csv("linear_models_genes_BasevsPBS_SI_summary.csv")


# Filter data for nonCF and CF
SI_Base_data <- Nano_normalized_BasevsBase_SI[Nano_normalized_BasevsBase_SI$Treatment == 'Baseline', ]
SI_PBS_data <- Nano_normalized_BasevsBase_SI[Nano_normalized_BasevsBase_SI$Treatment == 'PBS', ]

# Calculate median values for each cytokine in both Base and PBS groups
median_Base_SI <- aggregate(NormalizedCount ~ Gene, SI_Base_data, median)
median_PBS_SI <- aggregate(NormalizedCount ~ Gene, SI_PBS_data, median)


# Merge the two dataframes on cytokine names
foldchange_SI_genes <- merge(median_Base_SI, median_PBS_SI, by = "Gene")

# Calculate fold changes
foldchange_SI_genes$FoldChange <- foldchange_SI_genes$NormalizedCount.y / foldchange_SI_genes$NormalizedCount.x

# Rename columns for clarity
names(foldchange_SI_genes) <- c("Gene", "median_Base", "median_PBS", "FoldChange")


# Merge the 'P_Value_Genotype' column from 'linear_models_serum_summary' into 'foldchange_serum'
foldchange_SI_genes <- merge(foldchange_SI_genes, linear_models_genes_BasevsPBS_SI_summary[, c("Gene", "P_Value_Treatment")], by = "Gene", all.x = TRUE)



```

#Draw a volcano plot on Base vs PBS SI genes 
```{r}
# Transform the data
foldchange_SI_genes$log2FoldChange <- log2(foldchange_SI_genes$FoldChange)
foldchange_SI_genes$negLog10PValue <- -log10(foldchange_SI_genes$P_Value_Treatment)

# Add a new column "DiffExpression" to the foldchange_serum DataFrame
foldchange_SI_genes$DiffExpression <- ifelse(foldchange_SI_genes$P_Value_Treatment< 0.05, 
                                          ifelse(foldchange_SI_genes$FoldChange > 1, "Up in PBS", 
                                                 ifelse(foldchange_SI_genes$FoldChange < 1, "Down in PBS", "Not Significant")),
                                          "Not Significant")

# Biostatsquid theme
theme_set(theme_classic(base_size = 20) +
            theme(
              axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold", margin = margin(0,20,0,0), size = rel(0.5), color = 'black'),
              axis.title.x = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", margin = margin(20,0,0,0), size = rel(0.5), color = 'black'),
              plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
            ))


# Create a basic volcano plot
Volcanoplot_BasevsPBS_SI<- ggplot(data = foldchange_SI_genes, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = negLog10PValue, color = DiffExpression)) +
         geom_point()+
  theme_bw()+
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-0.6, 0.6), col = "gray", linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), col = "gray", linetype = 'dashed') +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Up in PBS" = "#00AFBB", "Down in PBS" = "#bb0c00", "Not Significant" = "grey"))+
  geom_text(aes(label=Gene), vjust = 2, hjust = 0.75, size = 2, check_overlap = TRUE)+
  ggtitle("Genes Differentially Expressed Between Base and PBS Gavaged Mice after LPS Challenge in Small Intestines")+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 12)) # Reduced font size for title

Volcanoplot_BasevsPBS_SI

# Save the plot as a PDF
ggsave("Volcanoplot_BasevsPBS_SI.pdf", plot = Volcanoplot_BasevsPBS_SI, width = 8, height = 4, device = "pdf")



```

#Determine which genes are differentially expressed across the gut
```{r}
#Subset only baseline data
Nano_normalized_Base <- subset(Nano_normalized_long_df, Treatment %in% c("Baseline"))
Nano_normalized_Base$MouseID <- paste(Nano_normalized_Base$Cage, Nano_normalized_Base$Mouse, sep = "_")

#Factor Treatment
Nano_normalized_Base$Biogeography <- factor(Nano_normalized_Base$Biogeography, levels = c("SI", "Cec", "Pro", "Dis"))


#Factor the Biogeography to be SI, Cec, Pro, and then Dis

###############

# Ensure the 'linear_models_gene' directory exists
dir.create("linear_models_diffgenes_acrossgut_atbaseline", showWarnings = FALSE)

# Initialize the summary dataframe
linear_models_diffgenes_baseline_summary <- data.frame(
  Gene = character(),
  P_Value_Biogeography_Cec = numeric(),
  P_Value_Biogeography_Pro = numeric(),
  P_Value_Biogeography_Dis = numeric(),
  P_Value_Sex = numeric(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Function to safely extract p-value
safe_extract_pvalue <- function(model_summary, term) {
  coef_table <- as.data.frame(model_summary$coefficients)
  if (term %in% rownames(coef_table)) {
    return(coef_table[term, "Pr(>|t|)"])
  }
  return(NA)
}

# Loop over each unique gene
for(gene in unique(Nano_normalized_Base$Gene)) {
  # Subset data for the current gene
  subset_data <- subset(Nano_normalized_Base, Gene == gene)

  # Fit the mixed linear model
  model <- lmerTest::lmer(log2(NormalizedCount) ~ Biogeography + Sex + (1|MouseID), data = subset_data)

  # Obtain the summary of the model
  model_summary <- summary(model)

    # Extract the p-values for Biogeography and Sex
  p_biogeography_cec <- safe_extract_pvalue(model_summary, "BiogeographyCec")
  p_biogeography_pro <- safe_extract_pvalue(model_summary, "BiogeographyPro")
  p_biogeography_dis <- safe_extract_pvalue(model_summary, "BiogeographyDis")
  p_sex <- safe_extract_pvalue(model_summary, "SexMale")

  # Add to the summary dataframe
  linear_models_diffgenes_summary <- rbind(linear_models_diffgenes_summary, data.frame(
    Gene = gene,
    P_Value_Biogeography_Cec = p_biogeography_cec,
    P_Value_Biogeography_Pro = p_biogeography_pro,
    P_Value_Biogeography_Dis = p_biogeography_dis,
    P_Value_Sex = p_sex
  ))

  # Save the summary of each model to a text file
  file_name <- paste0("linear_models_diffgenes_acrossgut_atbaseline/model_", gene, ".txt")
  capture.output(model_summary, file = file_name)
}

# View the summary dataframe
print(linear_models_diffgenes_summary)

# Function to categorize significance
categorize_significance <- function(p_value) {
  # Check if p_value is NA
  if (is.na(p_value)) {
    return("NA")
  }

  if (p_value < 0.001) {
    return("***")
  } else if (p_value < 0.01) {
    return("**")
  } else if (p_value < 0.05) {
    return("*")
  } else {
    return("NS")
  }
}

# Apply the function to the dataframe
linear_models_diffgenes_baseline_summary$Significance_Biogeography <- sapply(linear_models_diffgenes_baseline_summary$P_Value_Biogeography, categorize_significance)

linear_models_diffgenes_baseline_summary$Significance_Sex <- sapply(linear_models_diffgenes_baseline_summary$P_Value_Sex, categorize_significance)



# View the updated dataframe
linear_models_diffgenes_baseline_summary

# Apply Bonferroni correction to the P_Value_Treatment column
linear_models_diffgenes_baseline_summary$P_Value_Biogeography_Bonferroni <- p.adjust(linear_models_diffgenes_baseline_summary$P_Value_Biogeography, method = "bonferroni")

linear_models_diffgenes_baseline_summary$Significance_Sex_Bonferonni <- p.adjust(linear_models_diffgenes_baseline_summary$P_Value_Sex, method = "bonferroni")



#Add annotations and cell type to p value summary
linear_models_diffgenes_baseline_summary <- merge(linear_models_diffgenes_baseline_summary, annotations_df, by = "Gene")

# Save the dataframe as a CSV file
write.csv(linear_models_diffgenes_baseline_summary, "linear_models_diffgenes_baseline_summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)


```
#Make a heatmap
```{r}
#Reformat data to have all samples on x axis in order of importance (SI, Cec, Pro, Dis) and Treatment groups PBS and Bacteroides
#Merge genes that were significant only in each group

NormalizedHostResponseCounts_failedsamplesrm

#Dataframes
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_summary <- read_csv("linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_summary.csv")
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary <- read_csv("linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary.csv")
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary <- read_csv("linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary.csv")
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary <- read_csv("linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary.csv")

#filter significant genes in each excel
PBSvsBt_SI_sig_genes<- linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_summary %>%
  filter(P_Value_Treatment <= 0.01)

PBSvsBt_Cec_sig_genes<- linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary %>%
  filter(P_Value_Treatment <= 0.01)

PBSvsBt_Pro_sig_genes<- linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary %>%
  filter(P_Value_Treatment <= 0.01)

PBSvsBt_Dis_sig_genes<- linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary %>%
  filter(P_Value_Treatment <= 0.051)

#make a list of unique genes in each of these dataframes 

# Extracting unique genes from each dataframe
unique_genes_PBSvsBt_SI <- unique(PBSvsBt_SI_sig_genes$Gene)
unique_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec <- unique(PBSvsBt_Cec_sig_genes$Gene)
unique_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro <- unique(PBSvsBt_Pro_sig_genes$Gene)
unique_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis <- unique(PBSvsBt_Dis_sig_genes$Gene)

# Combine into a list
list_unique_genes <- list(
  SI = unique_genes_PBSvsBt_SI,
  Cec = unique_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec,
  Pro = unique_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro,
  Dis = unique_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis
)

# Create a dataframe for each region with unique genes
df_SI <- data.frame(Gene = unique_genes_PBSvsBt_SI, Region = "SmallIntestine")
df_Cec <- data.frame(Gene = unique_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec, Region = "Cecum")
df_Pro <- data.frame(Gene = unique_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro, Region = "ProximalColon")
df_Dis <- data.frame(Gene = unique_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis, Region = "DistalColon")

# Combine all dataframes into a single dataframe
combined_unique_genes_df <- bind_rows(df_SI, df_Cec, df_Pro, df_Dis)

#Remove "Region" and remmove duplicate genges
# Remove the "Region" column
combined_unique_genes_df <- combined_unique_genes_df %>% 
  select(-Region)

combined_unique_genes_df <- combined_unique_genes_df %>%
  distinct(Gene, .keep_all = TRUE)


# Get the unique ShortLabel values
unique_labels <- unique(Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt$ShortLabel)

# Loop over each unique ShortLabel
for(label in unique_labels) {
    # Subset the dataframe based on the current ShortLabel
    temp_df <- Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt[Nano_normalized_PBSvsBt$ShortLabel == label, ]

    # Keep only the desired columns
    temp_df <- temp_df[, c("Gene", "NormalizedCount")]
    
    colnames(temp_df)[colnames(temp_df) == "NormalizedCount"] <- label

    # Dynamically create a dataframe for each ShortLabel
    assign(paste0("df_", label), temp_df, envir = .GlobalEnv)
}

# Get a list of all the dataframe names created in the loop
df_names <- paste0("df_", unique_labels)

# Use mget to retrieve all dataframes from their names
dfs_to_merge <- mget(df_names, envir = .GlobalEnv)

# Merge all dataframes by 'Gene'
result_df <- Reduce(function(x, y) merge(x, y, by = "Gene", all = TRUE), dfs_to_merge)

# Subset result_df based on combined_unique_genes_df
result_df_filtered <- result_df %>%
                     filter(Gene %in% combined_unique_genes_df$Gene)

#Make gene the rownames
rownames(result_df_filtered) <- result_df_filtered$Gene

# Remove the now redundant 'Gene' column
result_df_filtered <- result_df_filtered[, -which(names(result_df_filtered) == "Gene")]

write.csv(result_df_filtered, "result_df_filtered2.csv", row.names = TRUE)


# List of ordered column names
ordered_columns <- c("3-2 SI", "5-2 SI", "11-2 SI",
                     "3-1 Cec", "3-2 Cec", "3-3 Cec", "5-1 Cec", "5-2 Cec", "5-3 Cec",
                    "9-1 Cec", "9-2 Cec", "9-3 Cec", "11-1 Cec", "11-2 Cec", "11-3 Cec",
                     "3-1 Pro", "3-2 Pro", "3-3 Pro", "5-1 Pro", "5-2 Pro", "5-3 Pro",
                     "9-1 Pro", "9-2 Pro", "9-3 Pro", "11-1 Pro", "11-2 Pro", "11-3 Pro",
                     "3-1 Dis", "3-2 Dis", "5-1 Dis", "5-3 Dis", 
                     "9-1 Dis", "9-2 Dis", "9-3 Dis", "11-1 Dis", "11-2 Dis", "11-3 Dis")


# Factor the column names
colnames(result_df_filtered)




# Creating the heatmap

cal_z_score <- function(x){
  (x - mean(x)) / sd(x)
}
 
data_subset_norm <- as.data.frame(t(apply(result_df_filtered, 1, cal_z_score)))
data_subset_norm <- data_subset_norm[ordered_columns]
pheatmap(data_subset_norm)

pheatmap(data_subset_norm, 
         scale = 'row', 
         cluster_cols = FALSE, 
         cluster_rows = TRUE)
```




#Make a venn diagram

```{r}
if (!require(devtools)) install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("yanlinlin82/ggvenn")

library(ggvenn)

linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_summary <- read_csv("linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_summary.csv")
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary <- read_csv("linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary.csv")
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary <- read_csv("linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary.csv")
linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary <- read_csv("linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary.csv")


# Extract genes with p-value <= 0.05 from each dataframe
genes_small_intestine <- linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_summary$Gene[linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_SI_summary$P_Value_Treatment <= 0.05]
genes_cecum <- linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary$Gene[linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Cec_summary$P_Value_Treatment <= 0.05]
genes_proximal_colon <- linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary$Gene[linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Pro_summary$P_Value_Treatment <= 0.05]
genes_distal_colon <- linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary$Gene[linear_models_genes_PBSvsBt_Dis_summary$P_Value_Treatment <= 0.05]


# Create a list of these gene sets
gene_sets <- list(
  "Small Intestine" = genes_small_intestine,
  "Cecum" = genes_cecum,
  "Proximal Colon" = genes_proximal_colon,
  "Distal Colon" = genes_distal_colon
)


# Generate the Venn diagram
venn.plot <- ggvenn(
  gene_sets,
  count = TRUE,
  show_elements = FALSE,
  show_percentage = FALSE,
  fill_color = c("darkblue", "royalblue", "lightblue", "#D1E8EE"))

venn.plot


# Save the plot as a PDF
ggsave("venn.plot.pdf", plot = venn.plot, width = 8, height = 4, device = "pdf")

#Find the gene that is reglated in the cecum, proximal colon, and dsital colon:

# Find common genes in Proximal Colon, and Distal Colon
common_Bt_genes <- Reduce(intersect, list(genes_proximal_colon, genes_distal_colon))

# Output the common genes
print(common_Bt_genes)


#make a df that has the genes in the venn diagram
unique_sets <- list(unique(genes_small_intestine), unique(genes_cecum), unique(genes_proximal_colon), unique(genes_distal_colon))

unique_gene_set_list <- unlist(unique_sets)
unique_gene_set_list <- as.character(unique_gene_set_list)

as.data.frame(venn_plot_genes)
venn_plot_genes$gene_names <- as.data.frame(unique_gene_set_list)


```


#Make a plot of Txk Expression along the gut and do stats
```{r}


# Subset Nano_normalized_long_df for the specified genes
Nano_normalized_long_Txk_df <- Nano_normalized_long_df %>%
  filter(Gene %in% "Txk")

pvb_treatment <- c("PBS", "Bacteroides")
pro_dis <- c("Pro", "Dis")

# Subset Nano_normalized_long_df for the specified treatments
Nano_normalized_long_Txk_df <- Nano_normalized_long_Txk_df %>%
  filter(Treatment %in% pvb_treatment)

Nano_normalized_long_Txk_df <- Nano_normalized_long_Txk_df %>%
  filter(Biogeography %in% pro_dis)


# Factor the 'Treatment' column with specific levels
Nano_normalized_long_Txk_df$Treatment <- factor(Nano_normalized_long_Txk_df$Treatment, 
                                                        levels = c("PBS", "Bacteroides"))
# Factor the 'Biogeography' column with specific levels
Nano_normalized_long_Txk_df$Biogeography <- factor(Nano_normalized_long_Txk_df$Biogeography, 
                                                        levels = c("Pro", "Dis"))

Nano_normalized_long_Txk_df$NormalizedCount <- as.numeric(Nano_normalized_long_Txk_df$NormalizedCount)



# Create the point plot


Nano_normalized_long_Txk_df_pointplot <- ggplot(Nano_normalized_long_Txk_df, aes( x=Treatment, y=NormalizedCount, color = Treatment, shape = Biogeography))+
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.25), size = 2) +  # Adjust position to separate points
  theme_bw()+
  facet_wrap(~Sex)+
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(7, 10)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c( "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange"))




ggsave("Nano_normalized_long_Txk_df_pointplott.pdf", plot = Nano_normalized_long_Txk_df_pointplot
, device = "pdf", width = 4, height = 2)




#Run stats Txk



  pvb_model_txk <- lm(log2(NormalizedCount) ~ Treatment + Sex + Treatment*Sex , data = Nano_normalized_long_Txk_df)
summary(pvb_model_txk)



```

#Make a plot of Atg12 Expression in the cecum and do stats
```{r}


# Subset Nano_normalized_long_df for the specified genes
Nano_normalized_long_Atg12_df <- Nano_normalized_long_df %>%
  filter(Gene %in% "Atg12")

pvb_treatment <- c("PBS", "Bacteroides")

# Subset Nano_normalized_long_df for the specified treatments and biogeography
Nano_normalized_long_Atg12_df <- Nano_normalized_long_Atg12_df %>%
  filter(Treatment %in% pvb_treatment,
         Biogeography %in% c("Cec"))


# Factor the 'Treatment' column with specific levels
Nano_normalized_long_Atg12_df$Treatment <- factor(Nano_normalized_long_Atg12_df$Treatment, 
                                                        levels = c("Baseline", "PBS", "Bacteroides"))

Nano_normalized_long_Atg12_df$NormalizedCount <- as.numeric(Nano_normalized_long_Atg12_df$NormalizedCount)



# Create the point plot


Nano_normalized_long_Atg12_df_pointplot <- ggplot(Nano_normalized_long_Atg12_df, aes( x=Treatment, y=NormalizedCount, color = Treatment, shape = Sex))+
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.25), size = 2) +  # Adjust position to separate points
  theme_bw()+
  facet_wrap(~Gene)+
  scale_color_manual(values = c( "Baseline"="lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange")) +
  geom_line(aes(group = interaction(Cage,Mouse)), size = 0.25) + # Add this line to connect points for each MouseID
  ylim(500, 1500)  # Set Y axis limits



Nano_normalized_long_Atg12_df_pointplot


ggsave("Nano_normalized_long_Atg12_df_pointplot_sextogether.pdf", plot = Nano_normalized_long_Atg12_df_pointplot
, device = "pdf", width = 3.2, height = 3)


#put black line around triangles with male: 

Nano_normalized_long_Atg12_df_pointplot_claude <- ggplot(Nano_normalized_long_Atg12_df, 
                                                    aes(x = Treatment, y = NormalizedCount)) +
  geom_point(data = subset(Nano_normalized_long_Atg12_df, Sex == "Female"),
             aes(shape = Sex, fill = Treatment),
             position = position_jitterdodge(dodge.width = 0.5, jitter.width = 1), size = 2,
             color = "grey50", stroke = 0.3) +
  geom_point(data = subset(Nano_normalized_long_Atg12_df, Sex == "Male"),
             aes(shape = Sex, fill = Treatment),
             position = position_jitterdodge(dodge.width = 0.5, jitter.width = 1), size = 2,
             color = "black", stroke = 0.8) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Female" = 21, "Male" = 24)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange"), guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange"), name = "Treatment") +
  theme_bw() +
  facet_wrap(~Gene) +
  ylim(500, 1500)

Nano_normalized_long_Atg12_df_pointplot_claude


ggsave("Nano_normalized_long_Atg12_df_pointplot_sextogether_PR.pdf", plot = Nano_normalized_long_Atg12_df_pointplot_claude
, device = "pdf", width = 3.2, height = 3)

#Run stats atg12



  pvb_model_atg12 <- lm(log2(NormalizedCount) ~ Treatment + Sex +Treatment*Sex, data = Nano_normalized_long_Atg12_df)
summary(pvb_model_atg12)




```

#make the plots for macrophage genes for paper
```{r}
#cd163
# Subset Nano_normalized_long_df for the specified genes
Nano_normalized_long_cd163_df <- Nano_normalized_long_df %>%
  filter(Gene %in% "Cd163")

pvb_treatment <- c("PBS", "Bacteroides")

# Subset Nano_normalized_long_df for the specified treatments and biogeography
Nano_normalized_long_cd163_df <- Nano_normalized_long_cd163_df %>%
  filter(Treatment %in% pvb_treatment,
         Biogeography %in% c("Cec"))


# Factor the 'Treatment' column with specific levels
Nano_normalized_long_cd163_df$Treatment <- factor(Nano_normalized_long_cd163_df$Treatment, 
                                                        levels = c("Baseline", "PBS", "Bacteroides"))

Nano_normalized_long_cd163_df$NormalizedCount <- as.numeric(Nano_normalized_long_cd163_df$NormalizedCount)



Nano_normalized_long_cd163_df_pointplot_claude <- ggplot(Nano_normalized_long_cd163_df, 
                                                    aes(x = Treatment, y = NormalizedCount)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = interaction(Cage, Mouse), color = Treatment), size = 0.25) +
  geom_point(data = subset(Nano_normalized_long_cd163_df, Sex == "Female"),
             aes(shape = Sex, fill = Treatment),
             position = position_jitterdodge(dodge.width = 0.5, jitter.width = 1), size = 2,
             color = "grey50", stroke = 0.3) +
  geom_point(data = subset(Nano_normalized_long_cd163_df, Sex == "Male"),
             aes(shape = Sex, fill = Treatment),
             position = position_jitterdodge(dodge.width = 0.5, jitter.width = 1), size = 2,
             color = "black", stroke = 0.8) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Female" = 21, "Male" = 24)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange"), guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange"), name = "Treatment") +
  theme_bw() +
  facet_wrap(~Gene)

Nano_normalized_long_cd163_df_pointplot_claude


ggsave("Nano_normalized_long_cd163_df_pointplot_sextogether_PR.pdf", plot = Nano_normalized_long_cd163_df_pointplot_claude
, device = "pdf", width = 3.2, height = 3)

#cd68
# Subset Nano_normalized_long_df for the specified genes
Nano_normalized_long_cd68_df <- Nano_normalized_long_df %>%
  filter(Gene %in% "Cd68")

pvb_treatment <- c("PBS", "Bacteroides")

# Subset Nano_normalized_long_df for the specified treatments and biogeography
Nano_normalized_long_cd68_df <- Nano_normalized_long_cd68_df %>%
  filter(Treatment %in% pvb_treatment,
         Biogeography %in% c("Cec"))


# Factor the 'Treatment' column with specific levels
Nano_normalized_long_cd68_df$Treatment <- factor(Nano_normalized_long_cd68_df$Treatment, 
                                                        levels = c("Baseline", "PBS", "Bacteroides"))

Nano_normalized_long_cd68_df$NormalizedCount <- as.numeric(Nano_normalized_long_cd68_df$NormalizedCount)



Nano_normalized_long_cd68_df_pointplot_claude <- ggplot(Nano_normalized_long_cd68_df, 
                                                    aes(x = Treatment, y = NormalizedCount)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = interaction(Cage, Mouse), color = Treatment), size = 0.25) +
  geom_point(data = subset(Nano_normalized_long_cd68_df, Sex == "Female"),
             aes(shape = Sex, fill = Treatment),
             position = position_jitterdodge(dodge.width = 0.5, jitter.width = 1), size = 2,
             color = "grey50", stroke = 0.3) +
  geom_point(data = subset(Nano_normalized_long_cd68_df, Sex == "Male"),
             aes(shape = Sex, fill = Treatment),
             position = position_jitterdodge(dodge.width = 0.5, jitter.width = 1), size = 2,
             color = "black", stroke = 0.8) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Female" = 21, "Male" = 24)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange"), guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange"), name = "Treatment") +
  theme_bw() +
  facet_wrap(~Gene)

Nano_normalized_long_cd68_df_pointplot_claude


ggsave("Nano_normalized_long_cd68_df_pointplot_sextogether_PR.pdf", plot = Nano_normalized_long_cd68_df_pointplot_claude
, device = "pdf", width = 3.2, height = 3)


#cd84
# Subset Nano_normalized_long_df for the specified genes
Nano_normalized_long_cd84_df <- Nano_normalized_long_df %>%
  filter(Gene %in% "Cd84")

pvb_treatment <- c("PBS", "Bacteroides")

# Subset Nano_normalized_long_df for the specified treatments and biogeography
Nano_normalized_long_cd84_df <- Nano_normalized_long_cd84_df %>%
  filter(Treatment %in% pvb_treatment,
         Biogeography %in% c("Cec"))


# Factor the 'Treatment' column with specific levels
Nano_normalized_long_cd84_df$Treatment <- factor(Nano_normalized_long_cd84_df$Treatment, 
                                                        levels = c("Baseline", "PBS", "Bacteroides"))

Nano_normalized_long_cd84_df$NormalizedCount <- as.numeric(Nano_normalized_long_cd84_df$NormalizedCount)



Nano_normalized_long_cd84_df_pointplot_claude <- ggplot(Nano_normalized_long_cd84_df, 
                                                    aes(x = Treatment, y = NormalizedCount)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = interaction(Cage, Mouse), color = Treatment), size = 0.25) +
  geom_point(data = subset(Nano_normalized_long_cd84_df, Sex == "Female"),
             aes(shape = Sex, fill = Treatment),
             position = position_jitterdodge(dodge.width = 0.5, jitter.width = 1), size = 2,
             color = "grey50", stroke = 0.3) +
  geom_point(data = subset(Nano_normalized_long_cd84_df, Sex == "Male"),
             aes(shape = Sex, fill = Treatment),
             position = position_jitterdodge(dodge.width = 0.5, jitter.width = 1), size = 2,
             color = "black", stroke = 0.8) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Female" = 21, "Male" = 24)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange"), guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange"), name = "Treatment") +
  theme_bw() +
  facet_wrap(~Gene)

Nano_normalized_long_cd84_df_pointplot_claude


ggsave("Nano_normalized_long_cd84_df_pointplot_sextogether_PR.pdf", plot = Nano_normalized_long_cd84_df_pointplot_claude
, device = "pdf", width = 3.2, height = 3)

#Ms4a4a
# Subset Nano_normalized_long_df for the specified genes
Nano_normalized_long_ms4a4a_df <- Nano_normalized_long_df %>%
  filter(Gene %in% "Ms4a4a")

pvb_treatment <- c("PBS", "Bacteroides")

# Subset Nano_normalized_long_df for the specified treatments and biogeography
Nano_normalized_long_ms4a4a_df <- Nano_normalized_long_ms4a4a_df %>%
  filter(Treatment %in% pvb_treatment,
         Biogeography %in% c("Cec"))


# Factor the 'Treatment' column with specific levels
Nano_normalized_long_ms4a4a_df$Treatment <- factor(Nano_normalized_long_ms4a4a_df$Treatment, 
                                                        levels = c("Baseline", "PBS", "Bacteroides"))

Nano_normalized_long_ms4a4a_df$NormalizedCount <- as.numeric(Nano_normalized_long_ms4a4a_df$NormalizedCount)



Nano_normalized_long_ms4a4a_df_pointplot_claude <- ggplot(Nano_normalized_long_ms4a4a_df, 
                                                    aes(x = Treatment, y = NormalizedCount)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = interaction(Cage, Mouse), color = Treatment), size = 0.25) +
  geom_point(data = subset(Nano_normalized_long_ms4a4a_df, Sex == "Female"),
             aes(shape = Sex, fill = Treatment),
             position = position_jitterdodge(dodge.width = 0.5, jitter.width = 1), size = 2,
             color = "grey50", stroke = 0.3) +
  geom_point(data = subset(Nano_normalized_long_ms4a4a_df, Sex == "Male"),
             aes(shape = Sex, fill = Treatment),
             position = position_jitterdodge(dodge.width = 0.5, jitter.width = 1), size = 2,
             color = "black", stroke = 0.8) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Female" = 21, "Male" = 24)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange"), guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange"), name = "Treatment") +
  theme_bw() +
  facet_wrap(~Gene)

Nano_normalized_long_ms4a4a_df_pointplot_claude


ggsave("Nano_normalized_long_ms4a4a_df_pointplot_sextogether_PR.pdf", plot = Nano_normalized_long_ms4a4a_df_pointplot_claude
, device = "pdf", width = 3.2, height = 3)
```



#Make a plot of Ikbke Expression in the proximal colon and do stats
```{r}


# Subset Nano_normalized_long_df for the specified genes
Nano_normalized_long_Ikbke_df <- Nano_normalized_long_df %>%
  filter(Gene %in% "Ikbke")

pvb_treatment <- c("PBS", "Bacteroides")

# Subset Nano_normalized_long_df for the specified treatments and biogeography
Nano_normalized_long_Ikbke_df <- Nano_normalized_long_Ikbke_df %>%
  filter(Treatment %in% pvb_treatment,
         Biogeography %in% c("Pro"))


# Factor the 'Treatment' column with specific levels
Nano_normalized_long_Ikbke_df$Treatment <- factor(Nano_normalized_long_Ikbke_df$Treatment, 
                                                        levels = c("Baseline", "PBS", "Bacteroides"))

Nano_normalized_long_Ikbke_df$NormalizedCount <- as.numeric(Nano_normalized_long_Ikbke_df$NormalizedCount)



# Create the point plot


Nano_normalized_long_Ikbke_df_pointplot <- ggplot(Nano_normalized_long_Ikbke_df, aes( x=Treatment, y=NormalizedCount, color = Treatment, shape = Sex))+
```
